---
title: "Semantic Representation and Tree-Structured Models"
chapter_id: "semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "treellm"
  - "spinoza"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "cgs"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Semantic Representation and Tree-Structured Models

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `treellm`, `spinoza`, `verification_bandwidth`, `cognitive_compilation`, `cgs` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs a place for explicit semantic representations that may improve grounding, explainability, and efficient reasoning.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Opaque token prediction does not by itself expose concept hierarchy, source grounding, or interpretable reasoning paths.

## Core Claim

[semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] Tree-structured semantic representations should be treated as optional representation substrates that must prove grounding, utility, and interoperability.

## Mechanism

- Represent concepts hierarchically.
- Attach semantic tokens to provenance and use constraints.
- Compare tree structures against neural-only baselines.

## Interfaces

- Spinoza uses semantic claims.
- VCM stores semantic pages.
- Compression tests representation efficiency.

## Invariants

- Semantic nodes are grounded or labeled speculative.
- Hierarchy changes are versioned.
- Representation claims require tests.

## Failure modes

- Hand-built ontology brittleness.
- False explainability.
- Hierarchy drift.

## Minimal implementation

A semantic-node schema and evaluation plan for one grounded domain.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Grounding fidelity test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Hierarchy revision test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Representation utility benchmark | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
